screening_results.reason: This is a narrative review on hemodynamic management of septic shock, not an original task-based fMRI study. It does not involve problem-solving tasks or healthy adult participants, and is not an fMRI study. Therefore it meets multiple exclusion criteria.
Abstract explicitly states this is a review article ('In this review...') and does not report original task-based fMRI results in healthy adult participants. Excluded per E1 (review/meta-analysis).

Abstract indicates this is a review of individual differences in working memory (correlational and theoretical review), not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It explicitly reviews literature rather than reporting new fMRI task activation results. Exclusion criteria met: review article (E1).

This article is a systematic review and meta-analysis of health literacy and aging, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1) and does not report fMRI task activation data (E4).

This is a narrative review about B cells and multiple sclerosis, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1), involves no fMRI data (E4), and does not include healthy adult participants (E3).

This is a surgical guideline/review paper on idiopathic normal-pressure hydrocephalus (patient population), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review/non-fMRI clinical paper and does not report task-related fMRI results in healthy adults.

The study is a functional connectivity (FCD) correlation analysis with Raven's scores and does not report a task-based fMRI paradigm or task-evoked activations. It appears to be a resting-state/connectivity-only study, so excluded per E2.

This is an ethics/education article for nurses about artificial nutrition/hydration, not a task-based fMRI study; it does not report task-related brain activation or involve healthy adult problem-solving participants. Therefore it meets exclusion criterion E4 (non-fMRI study).

Study reports a behavioral/developmental study of children (ages 3.6–6), not adults, and uses behavioral tests (TEC, Coloured Progressive Matrices, grammar), not task-based fMRI. It therefore does not meet I1 (task-based fMRI) or I3 (adult healthy sample) and should be excluded.

Event-related task-based fMRI study using a relational reasoning problem-solving task (adapted from Raven's matrices), includes a healthy adult group (ages 18-25), and reports task-related brain activation (PFC, RLPFC, parietal). All inclusion criteria are clearly met in the abstract.

Abstract describes behavioral experiments testing brief cognitive and social-psychological interventions on problem solving; there is no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or brain activation measures. Does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

This is a behavioral cognitive training trial (Mind Frontiers) with no mention of fMRI data acquisition or task-related brain activation results. It is therefore not a task-based fMRI study and should be excluded (E4: Non-fMRI studies).

This is a narrative review about lumbar puncture and CT indications, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review article and does not involve fMRI data or healthy adult task activation results.

This article is a review/qualitative synthesis of videogame training effects rather than a primary task-based fMRI study. It does not report original task-related fMRI activation in healthy adults, meeting the exclusion for reviews/meta-analyses (E1).

This is a book chapter/review on clinical diagnosis of work-related neurologic disorders and not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It does not report task-based fMRI results or include a problem-solving task.

Abstract lacks any mention of fMRI methods, task-based imaging, participant demographics, or specific problem-solving tasks. There is insufficient information to determine if this is a task-based fMRI study with healthy adults; include for full-text review to clarify eligibility.

This paper is a meta-analysis (review) of executive function in obstructive sleep apnea, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It therefore meets the exclusion criterion for reviews/meta-analyses.

Study examines children (ages 8–16) with ADHD using behavioral/neuropsychological tests; no healthy adult sample and no task-based fMRI reported. Does not meet the review’s focus on task-based fMRI in healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks.
Study involves children (ages 5–12), not adults, and describes behavioral assessment without any fMRI data; therefore it does not meet the fMRI or adult-sample criteria for inclusion.

This is a review/risk–benefit assessment of hyaluronan injections for osteoarthritis (clinical treatment), not a task-based fMRI study. It involves patients with osteoarthritis rather than healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI study, so it fails the review’s inclusion criteria.

This is an in vitro cellular/molecular study of prostate cancer cells, not a task-based fMRI study with human participants performing problem-solving tasks. It has no healthy adult sample and is not fMRI. Exclusion criteria E4 and E3 apply.
Participants were 4-year-old children (no healthy adult sample) and the study did not use fMRI (behavioral training outcomes), so it fails the adult-sample and fMRI requirements.

Abstract describes a behavioral study of planning using the Tower of London in healthy aging with no mention of task-based fMRI or reported brain activation. This is a non-fMRI study, so it meets exclusion criterion E4.
Abstract describes a theoretical/teaching physiology article without any fMRI data or human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and it does not report task-related brain activation.

Abstract describes a task-based fMRI study of fluid reasoning that reports task-related BOLD activations. The sample’s health status/age range is not specified in the abstract, so include for full-text review to confirm presence of healthy adults (18–65).
Participants were children aged 7–9 (no healthy adult sample) and the intervention involved behavioral training with no fMRI; thus the study fails the adult sample and fMRI criteria (E3, E4).

This is a diffusion tensor imaging (structural/white-matter) study in mathematically gifted adolescents, not a task-based fMRI study in healthy adults; it reports white-matter microstructure/connectivity rather than task-related activations.
This is a basic science/gene delivery study in mice, not a task-based human fMRI study. It involves animal models and molecular biology, so no healthy adult participants and not an fMRI study.
Participants are children (ages 8–12); no healthy adult (18–65) sample, so study does not meet population criterion for the review.

This is a behavioral genetic study in 12-year-old twins using cognitive tests (VESPARCH), not a task-based fMRI study, and participants are children (age 12) rather than healthy adults. Therefore it fails the fMRI requirement and the adult sample requirement.
Abstract describes a behavioral latent-variable study with span and reasoning tests; no mention of fMRI or any task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study.
Abstract describes behavioral working-memory training and transfer results; no mention of fMRI, task-based imaging, or reported brain activation. This is a non-fMRI study and thus excluded.

Event-related task-based fMRI study in humans performing semantic and analogical reasoning (problem-solving). Includes a young adult group and reports task-related brain activation (age-related changes in VLPFC, RLPFC). Meets all inclusion criteria.

This is a radiology checklist/article about HRCT interpretation of lung parenchymal abnormalities, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task-related brain activation.

The abstract describes behavioral experiments validating a relational integration task and modeling its relation to fluid reasoning, with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging methods, or task-related brain activation. Thus it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

The article is a historical/theoretical review of concepts of mind, not an original task-based fMRI study. It has no participants, no problem-solving task, and reports no task-related brain activation results.

This is a commentary/reanalysis of behavioral latent-variable studies, not an original task-based fMRI study. The abstract reports behavioral correlations and theoretical discussion, with no task-based fMRI or brain activation results. Excluded for being a review/commentary and non-fMRI.

Task-based fMRI study of human visuospatial relational reasoning reporting task-related activations. However participants are aged 8–19 and the abstract does not specify a distinct adult (18–65) group; include for full-text review to verify adult sample.

This is a commentary/reanalysis/meta-analytic critique, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. The abstract describes a reanalysis of correlations between working memory and intelligence, with no fMRI methods or reported task-related brain activation.

This article is a narrative review about artificial nutrition and hydration for terminally ill patients, not an experimental task-based fMRI study. It does not involve problem-solving tasks in healthy adults nor report task-related brain activation. Therefore it meets multiple exclusion criteria.

This is a clinical review/article on fluid management in pre-eclampsia, not a task-based fMRI study. It involves pregnant patients (not healthy adults) and appears to be a review/clinical algorithm rather than original fMRI research. Excluded per E1, E3, and E4.

Study involves children and neuropsychological testing, not task-based fMRI in healthy adults. Participants are pediatric (not 18–65), and no indication of fMRI methods or task-based brain activation reporting.

The study reports correlations between Raven scores and regional gray matter volume and ReHo across healthy subjects, not task-based fMRI activation during a problem-solving task. It is a resting-state/structural correlation study rather than a task-based activation study.

This is a literature review on clinically assisted hydration in dying patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1), includes no healthy adult sample (E3), and is not an fMRI study (E4).
Abstract describes behavioral experiments (modified n-back) and reports cognitive variance in reasoning; no mention of fMRI or any neuroimaging. Thus this is a non-fMRI study.

The abstract describes theoretical/biological work on organ morphogenesis in non-human tissue and does not report any fMRI methods, human participants, or task-related brain activation. It therefore fails the fMRI and healthy adult criteria.

The study sample consists of adolescents (math-gifted and controls) rather than adults. Therefore it does not include a healthy adult (18–65) group and meets exclusion criterion E3. Although it is a task-based fMRI study reporting task-related activations, the absence of adult participants requires exclusion.

The study is a clinical discussion/review of lymphedema and wound care with no mention of fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or healthy adult neuroimaging. It is therefore a non-fMRI clinical paper and does not meet the review objective.

Not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. This is a theoretical/modeling paper on communicating hydrocephalus (neurosurgery), with no fMRI task, no problem-solving task, and no healthy adult sample reported.

The abstract describes a nephrology review/opinion about peritoneal dialysis and residual renal function in CKD patients—not a task-based fMRI experiment. It involves a patient population (not healthy adults) and is a non‑fMRI review, so exclusion is clear.

Abstract reports task-related brain activations during nonverbal reasoning experiments in humans, addressing problem-solving/reasoning. It does not explicitly state fMRI modality or participant ages/health, so include for full-text review to confirm imaging method and presence of healthy adult sample.

This is a narrative review of cognitive and behavioral features of Costello syndrome (a clinical population), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review article, involves a clinical sample rather than healthy adults, and does not report task-related fMRI activation results.

The study reports structural MRI and cognitive testing in hypertensive vs normotensive adults, not task-based fMRI and does not report task-related brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review criterion for task-based fMRI.

Narrative review on sex differences and educational implications; not an empirical task-based fMRI study, contains no report of task-related brain activation or healthy adult fMRI participants. Therefore it is a review and a non-fMRI paper.
Task-based fMRI study of Raven's Progressive Matrices problem solving in seven young, healthy participants reporting task-related brain activation results, meeting all inclusion criteria.
This is a laboratory study of lactate production in myometrial tissue, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving; it is a non-fMRI, biochemical/in vitro study.

This study used transcranial alternating current stimulation (tACS) and behavioral performance on Raven's matrices, not task-based fMRI. It is a non-fMRI neuromodulation study, so it does not meet the fMRI requirement.

This is a task-based fMRI study using the Raven's Standard Progressive Matrices (a reasoning/problem-solving task), reports task-related brain activations (BAs 18, 9, 7), and includes a non-autistic control group (healthy adults).

The abstract describes psychometric evaluation of selection tests in junior doctors with no mention of fMRI or any brain imaging; therefore this is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the meta-analysis objective.
Abstract reports a behavioral/psychometric study with no mention of fMRI or neuroimaging. Does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement for the review.

The abstract describes a behavioral study relating casual video game performance to cognitive task scores; it makes no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation results. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the inclusion criteria.

This is an ethical/legal discussion about artificial nutrition and hydration, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task-related brain activation or include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks.

The study examines sleep, cognitive impairment, and depression in maintenance hemodialysis patients (clinical patient sample) with no mention of task-based fMRI or problem-solving tasks. Thus it does not include healthy adult participants nor fMRI methods.

The study involves children/adolescents (ages 12–15), not healthy adults, and is a behavioral longitudinal study without fMRI imaging. Therefore it does not meet the adult fMRI task-based inclusion criteria.

Abstract describes behavioral measures and factor analyses of executive function and reasoning without any mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation results—does not meet fMRI requirement.

This is a behavioral psychometric study of the Wonderlic and Woodcock-Johnson in college students; no fMRI or task-related brain activation is reported. Does not meet the imaging requirement for the review.

Abstract describes neurodevelopmental outcomes after pediatric cardiac surgery in infants/children; not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants, and does not report task-related fMRI activations. Exclude based on no healthy adult sample and non-fMRI focus.

This is a historical review of renal medicine in the 18th century, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. It does not involve fMRI data, problem-solving tasks, or healthy adult participants. It is a review article and non-fMRI, so clearly excluded.

This is a policy/governance article about NICE decision-making and deliberation. It is not a task-based fMRI study, does not report human problem-solving tasks or task-related brain activation, and contains no neuroimaging data. Therefore it does not meet the review inclusion criteria.

Abstract describes computerized clinical decision-support protocols for critical care (mechanical ventilation, fluids) and is not an fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. No brain imaging, task-based fMRI, or healthy adult problem-solving task is reported in the abstract.

This is an educational/review-style article about mathematics in microbiology, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. It reports no human participants performing problem-solving tasks or task-related brain activation. Therefore it meets exclusions for reviews/non-empirical work, non-fMRI methods, and no healthy adult sample.

The abstract reports cognitive performance in MDMA users but makes no mention of fMRI, task-based imaging, or reported task-related brain activation. It appears to be a non-fMRI behavioral/neurochemical study and thus does not meet the review objective.

Abstract describes behavioral/psychometric tasks (Block Design, Matrix Reasoning, reaction time, Wisconsin Card Sorting, Tower of London) with no mention of fMRI or neuroimaging; thus it is a non-fMRI study (E4).

This is an open trial of an attention-training intervention in school-aged children with ADHD, not a task-based fMRI study, and it does not include healthy adult participants. Therefore it fails the fMRI and healthy adult criteria.

This study is a clinical longitudinal assessment of cognitive abilities in patients with Costello syndrome; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants, and reports behavioral/cognitive test results rather than task-related brain activation.

This is a nutrition intervention trial in Indian schoolchildren assessing growth and cognition, not a task-based fMRI study in healthy adults and does not report fMRI activation results.

This is an educational survey of undergraduate global/public health programs at liberal arts colleges, not a task-based fMRI experiment. It includes no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no brain activation data.

Abstract describes behavioral task-switching and cognitive relations; no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or brain activation results. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the task-based fMRI inclusion criteria.

Abstract reads like a commentary/news or review summarizing a neuroimaging finding rather than reporting an original task-based fMRI experiment. No methods, participants, tasks, or task-related activation results are provided. Trends in Cognitive Sciences commonly publishes reviews/commentaries, so exclude as a review.

Abstract describes longitudinal cognitive testing (Raven's, Logical Memory) in older adults; no mention of fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

Abstract reports task-based fMRI during a reversal learning task, includes healthy adult participants (n=28), and reports task-related BOLD activation related to prediction errors and fluid intelligence/reasoning. Reversal learning involves flexible problem solving; include for full-text review to confirm alignment with problem-solving task definitions.

Task-based fMRI with 106 human participants (ages 20–77) performing fluid reasoning and perceptual speed tasks; reports task-related activation maps and links behavioral to topographic similarity. Includes healthy adults in the 18–65 range and directly addresses problem-solving/reasoning. Meets all inclusion criteria.

Study is a behavioral psychometric study in children (Grades 1–5), not adults, and reports no task-based fMRI data. Does not include healthy adult participants and is a non-fMRI study, so it fails the review criteria.

The abstract describes a web-based cognitive assessment conducted remotely with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.
Abstract describes a behavioral study of adolescents (not adults) and does not mention fMRI or neuroimaging methods. Therefore it lacks a healthy adult sample and is not an fMRI study.

This is a behavioral sleep study investigating anagram solving after REM/NREM awakenings; the abstract does not mention task-based fMRI or report task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and excluded.

This study reports β-amyloid imaging and cognitive performance in healthy aging, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. The abstract does not describe fMRI task activation or problem-solving tasks, so it fails the fMRI requirement.

Task-based fMRI study using Raven’s Progressive Matrices (a reasoning/problem-solving task) with autistic and non-autistic (healthy adult) participants; reports task-related brain activation and connectivity, meeting all inclusion criteria.
Screening failed: [WinError 5] Access is denied: 'outputs\\abstract_screening_results.json_lock'
Animal (rat) experimental study, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving; no healthy adult human participants and not an fMRI study reporting task-related brain activation.

This is a longitudinal structural MRI study in older adults (ages ~68–73), not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults aged 18–65. It reports structural measures and cognitive correlations, not task-related fMRI activation results.

Abstract describes a behavioral study of social reasoning using stories and coding; no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or reported task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet imaging criteria.

The study is a pharmacological resting-state fMRI connectivity study in healthy subjects and does not describe any task-based problem-solving or reasoning fMRI paradigm. It therefore fails the requirement for task-based activation and should be excluded.

Task-based fMRI study where adults performed a modified Raven's Progressive Matrices (problem-solving/reasoning). Includes a healthy adult control group and reports task-related brain activation results, meeting all inclusion criteria.

This study is a clinical treatment study of bevacizumab for radiation necrosis, not a task-based fMRI investigation. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks or report task-related fMRI activations. Therefore it does not meet the review objectives.

This is a longitudinal behavioral study of children/adolescents with no mention of fMRI; sample does not include healthy adults (18–65). Therefore it fails the fMRI requirement and the adult-sample requirement.

Behavioral study of social support and cognition in older African American adults (mean age 69). It does not use fMRI or report task-related brain activation, and the sample is outside the target healthy adult age range (18–65). Does not meet inclusion criteria for task-based fMRI problem-solving studies.

Task-based fMRI study using Raven’s progressive matrices (reasoning/problem-solving) with two human groups (high-IQ N=8, low-IQ N=10), reports task-related cortical activation differences. Meets all inclusion criteria and no exclusion criteria apply.

The study is not an fMRI study: it measures self-report, EMG, heart rate, and electrodermal activity. The task is emotion regulation (reappraisal), not a problem-solving/reasoning task. Therefore it does not meet the meta-analysis focus on task-based fMRI of problem solving.

This is a genetic association study of face cognition and fluid abilities with no mention of task-based fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. It does not investigate problem-solving tasks in fMRI. Exclude as a non-fMRI study.
This is a behavioral study using Navon, Remote Associates, and Alternate Uses tasks with no task-based fMRI or reported brain activation; thus it is a non-fMRI study.

Abstract describes behavioral experiments on analogy generation and transfer to a picture-mapping task; no mention of fMRI or any neuroimaging methods. Does not meet inclusion criterion I1 (task-based fMRI). Exclude under E4 (Non-fMRI study).

This is not a task-based fMRI study: it uses MRI and 1H-MR spectroscopy and reports imaging in patient groups (MIDD and T1D controls), not healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks; no task-related fMRI activation results are reported.

Task-based fMRI with cognitive tasks including a fluid reasoning (problem-solving) domain; includes healthy adult participants (ages 20–80, includes 18–65 range); reports task-related activation results (GLM analyses and component prediction accuracies).

Study reports behavioral/neuropsychological assessment (NEPSY) in children with spina bifida, not a task-based fMRI study, and does not include healthy adult participants. No task-related brain activation reported.

Abstract describes structural T1-weighted MRI measures (cortical thickness, subcortical volumes) and cognitive composites, not task-based fMRI. No task-related brain activation or problem-solving fMRI paradigm is reported, so this is a non-fMRI study.

Behavioral longitudinal study of older adults (ages 64–92) examining cognitive test variability; no task-based fMRI or brain activation results reported, and sample is outside the 18–65 healthy adult range required.

Abstract reports a behavioral psychometric study of children (ages 6–12) using K-ABC and DAS, not an fMRI study, and does not include healthy adults. Exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI study) and E3 (no healthy adult sample) apply.

The study is a behavioral/developmental assessment in children aged 4–7, not task-based fMRI and includes no adult participants. It therefore does not meet inclusion criteria I1–I3 and clearly meets exclusion criteria E3 (no healthy adult sample) and E4 (non-fMRI study).

The study reports behavioral testing (Raven's matrices) in children with Asperger's and matched typically developing children. It is not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants, so it fails inclusion criteria for fMRI and adult sample.

This is an animal (mouse) study of nasal development using surgical manipulation and computational fluid dynamics, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It has no healthy adult human participants and is not an fMRI study, so it fails inclusion criteria and meets exclusion criteria E3 and E4.

This study is not an fMRI study: it reports behavioral and physiological measures (cognition tests, mood, balance, temperatures) with no brain imaging or task-related activation reported, so it fails the fMRI requirement.

Abstract describes psychometric/structural modeling of emotional intelligence in a sample of students; no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, task-based activation, or problem-solving tasks. Therefore it is not an fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.
This is a behavioral psychometric study of the Everyday Problem Solving Test in older African-American adults with no mention of fMRI or task-related brain activation; not a task-based fMRI study.
The study participants are high school students (adolescents), so no healthy adult (18–65) sample is reported; therefore it fails inclusion criterion I3.

Abstract reports behavioral studies of emotion recognition in healthy elderly, not task-based fMRI, and participants are elderly (likely >65) rather than the target 18–65 adult group. Does not report fMRI activation or problem-solving tasks. Exclude based on non-fMRI methodology and absence of eligible adult sample.

This is a conceptual/review article on the evolution and comparative literature of working memory, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. The abstract does not report fMRI methods, healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks, or task-related activation results.

This study is not task-based fMRI and does not report brain activation. It concerns dietetic diagnostic codes in hemodialysis (ESRD) patients, not healthy adult participants, so it fails inclusion criteria.

This is a behavioral study of predispositional engagement and activity in older adults; the abstract does not mention any task-based fMRI or report task-related brain activation, and the sample is older adults (outside the 18–65 target range). Does not meet fMRI or target-age requirements.

Study examines NREM sleep/CAP and cognitive performance in healthy children using sleep measures; not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adults. Fails inclusion criteria I1 and I3. Exclusion criteria E3 (no healthy adult sample) and E4 (non-fMRI study) apply.

The study reports behavioral/cognitive assessments in older adults (ages 70–103) with no mention of fMRI or brain imaging. Therefore it does not include a healthy adult sample in the 18–65 range and is a non-fMRI study.
This is an animal (rat) physiological study of thiazide effects on renal interstitial calcium, not a task-based fMRI, and it includes no healthy adult human participants or brain activation results.

Animal (rat) study measuring HPA responses to endotoxin/IL-1β; not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. No healthy adult participants and not an fMRI study, so it does not meet inclusion criteria.

Abstract discusses assessment of cognitive abilities in the elderly and dementia; it does not report any task-based fMRI methods or problem-solving tasks and targets older adults (not the 18–65 healthy adult sample). Therefore non-fMRI and wrong age group.

The study is a longitudinal psychometric/behavioral analysis of cognitive abilities using the Woodcock-Johnson battery; no task-based fMRI or reported brain activation is described. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study not meeting the review objective.

This is a review/book chapter on computational methods in biochemical/biomedical engineering and bioinformatics, not a task-based fMRI experiment. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks or report task-related brain activation.

This is a genetic association and behavioral study with structural MRI in an elderly cohort; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not report task-related fMRI activation results relevant to problem-solving in healthy adults 18–65.

The study reports task-based fMRI (BOLD) during reasoning/problem-solving in a sample of healthy young adults (n=225), and presents task-related brain activation results and structure-function associations. All inclusion criteria are met based on the abstract.

This is a longitudinal behavioral study of spoken discourse memory in older adults; no task-based fMRI was reported. Participants are older adults (not a healthy 18–65 adult sample). Therefore it does not meet the fMRI requirement and the target age group.

This is a biomarker/postmortem CSF and brain tissue study in Alzheimer's disease, not a task-based fMRI experiment. No task performance or task-related fMRI activation in healthy adults is reported; samples are postmortem/clinical and aged controls. Fails inclusion criteria and clearly meets exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI study) and E3 (no healthy adult 18-65 sample).

Abstract describes a behavioral working memory training study with pre/post behavioral assessments and no mention of fMRI, imaging methods, or task-related brain activation results. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review's imaging requirement.
Study is an educational simulation trial for medical students with no mention of fMRI or neuroimaging methods; therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not meet inclusion.

Abstract reports a longitudinal behavioral study of personality, activity, and cognitive tests in older adults (ages 64–68). No mention of fMRI, task-based imaging, or task-related brain activation results. Therefore excluded as a non-fMRI study (E4).

Participants are boys (children) with clinical diagnoses; no healthy adult (18–65) sample is included. Study reports neuropsychological test-retest results, not task-based fMRI data. Excluded for lack of healthy adult sample and non-fMRI methods.

Abstract describes a behavioural study of working memory and performance pressure with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging methods, or reported brain activation results; therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study.

Abstract describes a behavioral cognitive-training study with no mention of fMRI, task-related brain activation, or neuroimaging methods. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the meta-analysis objective.

Task-based fMRI study of 79 healthy subjects performing Raven's Progressive Matrices (a reasoning/problem-solving task); reports task-related network activations and spatial/functional connectivity changes consistent with P-FIT, meeting the review objective.

Abstract describes a behavioral virtual-reality study of learning spatial intuitions with no mention of fMRI, imaging methods, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet inclusion criteria.
Abstract describes a behavioral ToM task with inhibitory manipulations in young and old adults; no mention of fMRI methods or task-related brain activation. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study.

The study involves 9–10-year-old children and reports behavioral cognitive assessments without any fMRI methods. It therefore does not include a healthy adult sample (E3) and is a non-fMRI study (E4).

Participants are adolescents (mean age 16.5), not adults aged 18–65, so the study does not include a healthy adult sample required by the review. Although it is task-based fMRI of reasoning with reported activations, it fails I3.

This is a lesion-based neuropsychological study of patients (no task-based fMRI) and does not include a healthy adult sample. It therefore fails inclusion criteria I1 and I3 and meets exclusion criteria E4 and E3.

This is a computational/formal modelling paper about anatomical connectivity and physiological transport of proteins. It is not an fMRI study, includes no human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related brain activation.
This is an engineering methods paper on wastewater treatment monitoring using MPCA and CBR—no human participants or fMRI data; does not study problem-solving in healthy adults.

This is a clinical case report of an infected patient; it is not an fMRI study nor a task-based problem-solving study and does not include healthy adult participants. Exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI) and E3 (no healthy adult sample) apply.

Participants are 4th–5th grade children (not healthy adults 18–65), and the study reports behavioral/cognitive assessments without any fMRI methods. Therefore it does not meet the adult sample or fMRI requirements.

Abstract describes behavioral study of pitch perception and memory in school-aged children with autism, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. Participants are children and no fMRI methods or task-related brain activation are reported.

This is a diffusion tensor MRI structural study relating white-matter indices to fluid intelligence, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not report task-related fMRI activation results.
Abstract describes behavioral studies of crossword puzzle proficiency in adults with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or brain activation results. Does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

This study reports behavioral and peripheral physiological measures (salivary cortisol, blood pressure, heart rate) during cognitive tasks but does not use fMRI or report brain activation. Participants are elderly (65–80), so no healthy adult (18–65) sample. Therefore it fails inclusion criteria I1 and I3 (meets exclusion criteria E4 and E3).

This is a behavioral study of expertise in Go players with no mention of fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or brain activation results. It is therefore a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review's imaging requirement.

Abstract describes a behavioral neuropsychological study using the Tower of London and WAIS subtests with no mention of fMRI methods or task-related brain activation results; therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study.

This is a single clinical case report of a patient with metastatic prostate cancer and suspected neuroborreliosis; it is not an fMRI study, does not include healthy adult participants, and reports clinical outcomes rather than task-related brain activation.

Task-based event-related fMRI study in 12 healthy right-handed adults performing fluid analogy problem-solving; reports task-related BOLD activations and correlations with intelligence, satisfying all inclusion criteria.

This is a qualitative social research study of commuting using interviews and photo-elicitation; it does not use fMRI or any brain imaging, nor does it report task-related brain activation. Does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

This study uses neuropsychological testing (WJ-III CHC-based measures) to differentiate Alzheimer's and depression in elderly clinical populations. The abstract does not report any task-based fMRI methods or include healthy adult participants, so it fails the fMRI and healthy-adult criteria.

Abstract describes task-based fMRI with task-related activations, but the task (time estimation with feedback) is not clearly a problem-solving or reasoning task and participant ages/health are not specified. Include for full-text review to confirm whether I2 and I3 are met.

This is a historical/anatomical article examining specimens, not an experimental task-based fMRI study. It does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks nor report task-related brain activation.

The fMRI task is a memory encoding task, not a problem-solving or reasoning task (I2 not met). The study is task-based fMRI with healthy adults and reports task activation (I1, I3, I4 met), but it does not address problem solving, so excluded from this review.

This study is a physiologic investigation of microvascular fluid filtration using venous plethysmography and tilt; it is not an fMRI study nor does it examine task-based brain activation or problem-solving. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI inclusion criteria.

This is a clinical case report of a single patient with neurosyphilis; no task-based fMRI was performed, no healthy adult sample is included, and no task-related brain activation results are reported.

The study is molecular immunology using latently infected B cell lines, not an fMRI study or human problem-solving task. It includes no healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI investigation of problem solving.

This is an animal (mouse) study of lung regeneration using cellular assays and modeling; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve human adult participants performing problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related brain activation.

Abstract describes an engineering/visualisation system for wastewater treatment; no fMRI methods or human problem-solving task reported. No healthy adult participants. Not relevant to task-based fMRI of problem solving.

Abstract describes an online randomized cognitive training trial focused on behavioral cognitive outcomes; it does not mention fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or reporting of task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review's fMRI inclusion requirement.

The abstract describes a mathematical model of thrombin generation (coagulation) and CFD use; it is not an fMRI study, does not involve humans performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and outside the review scope.

This is a behavioral cognitive study examining working memory and fluid intelligence; the abstract contains no indication of task-based fMRI or any neuroimaging results. It therefore fails the fMRI requirement for inclusion.

Study involves typically developing young children (48–77 months), not healthy adults, and does not use fMRI (behavioral/cognitive assessments). Therefore it fails the adult-sample and fMRI inclusion requirements.

This is a diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) structural MRI study of white-matter microstructure before and after reasoning training. It is not a task-based fMRI study and does not report task-related functional activation, so it meets exclusion criterion E4 (Non-fMRI studies).

This is a medication trial in elementary-school children with ADHD assessing reading performance, not a task-based fMRI study, and it does not include healthy adult participants (ages 18–65). Thus it fails inclusion criteria I1/I2/I3 and meets exclusion criteria E3 and E4.

This study describes psychometric development of abbreviated Raven's matrices; it is not a task-based fMRI study and reports no neuroimaging or brain activation results. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI-related inclusion criteria.

The paper describes an engineering/case-based system for supervising an activated sludge wastewater treatment process, not an fMRI study. It involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no brain activation results, so it fails inclusion criteria.

The study is an immunology investigation of cerebrospinal fluid T cells, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI or cognitive task brain activation, so it fails inclusion and meets exclusion E4 (non-fMRI study).

This is an immunology/virology study on human rhinovirus and airway epithelial responses (likely in children), not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem-solving in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI or adult healthy participants.

Qualitative interview and think-aloud study of nurses’ clinical decision-making; no fMRI or neuroimaging was used and no brain activation results are reported. Does not meet task-based fMRI requirements (I1/I4).
This is an animal physiology study of shark rectal gland, not a human task-based fMRI study; it involves no healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI study of problem solving.

This is a behavioral/clinical longitudinal study of individuals with Costello syndrome using standardized cognitive and adaptive testing. It is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult problem-solving tasks, and therefore fails inclusion criteria I1–I3.

The study reports behavioral/psychometric testing in secondary school students with no mention of fMRI or neuroimaging, and the sample are secondary school students (not adults). Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include a healthy adult (18-65) sample.

Study enrolled adolescents (ages 10–16) with fragile X syndrome rather than healthy adults, and used behavioral cognitive testing (Leiter-R) in a longitudinal design; no task-based fMRI or task-related brain activation results are reported.

This study is a proteomic analysis of synovial fluid in osteoarthritis patients and controls; it is not an fMRI study and does not involve task-based problem-solving in healthy adults. Therefore it meets the exclusion criterion for non-fMRI studies.
This is a longitudinal observational study of behavioral/cognitive measures; no fMRI or brain activation/task-based imaging is reported. It does not meet the requirement for a task-based fMRI study.

This is a longitudinal behavioral study of pediatric cochlear implant recipients using cognitive tests and parent-report measures; it does not use task-based fMRI and the participants are children (not healthy adults). Therefore it fails the fMRI (E4) and healthy adult sample (E3) criteria.
Animal (guinea pig) study of Pseudomonas virulence factors; not a task-based fMRI study in humans performing problem-solving tasks. Therefore no healthy adult participants and not an fMRI study.

This is a review article on surface phase transitions in physical systems, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves no human participants or fMRI data, and is explicitly a review.

Task-based fMRI study of fluid reasoning (Visualization and Induction), meeting objectives of problem-solving/reasoning tasks. Abstract does not specify participant ages/health or report standard task-activation maps (focuses on connectivity), so full-text needed to confirm healthy adult sample and activation reporting.

This is a microbiological study of dialysis fluid culture methods, not a task-based fMRI investigation. It does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks or report brain activation results, so it is not relevant to the review.

Study is behavioral cohort research in older adults (ages 70–79), not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adults aged 18–65. Therefore it does not meet imaging or target population criteria for the review.

Abstract describes behavioral experiments on belief-based vs analytic processing in transitive inference with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation results; thus it is a non-fMRI study.

This is a preclinical animal study using multispectral optical imaging in rats; it is not an fMRI study and includes no human participants or problem-solving tasks. Does not meet the review’s task-based fMRI in healthy adults requirement.

The study reports structural MRI (hippocampal volume), genetics, and behavioral correlations, not task-based fMRI activation during a problem-solving task. It is therefore a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

This is a developmental psychology study of life narratives with children and young adults; it does not use fMRI or report task-related brain activation and is not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving.

The abstract describes palliative wound care in patients with malignant fungating wounds, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It involves a patient (clinical) sample rather than healthy adults and does not report fMRI or brain activation results.

This is an animal (rat) hemorrhage/resuscitation study involving methylene blue and anesthetics—no human participants, not a task-based fMRI study, and no task-related brain activation reported. It does not meet the review’s fMRI/problem-solving/healthy adult criteria.

Abstract describes behavioral/psychometric analyses (analytical inference, simulations) and working memory tasks with a large sample; no mention of fMRI methods or task-related brain activation. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

This is an engineering paper about an expert system for industrial anaerobic digestion, not a task-based fMRI study. It involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no fMRI brain activation results.

This is an engineering/technical demonstration of an environmental control life support system, not a task-based fMRI study. It involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no brain activation results.

This is a fieldwork/methods report of a school-based HIV prevention intervention among South African adolescents; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not include healthy adults (18-65), and does not report task-related brain activation. Excluded for non-fMRI study and no healthy adult sample.

This study investigates toxoplasma infection risk in slaughterhouse workers and is an epidemiological/occupational health study, not a task-based fMRI problem-solving study. It clearly is a non-fMRI study, so it does not meet the review objective.

This paper describes a decision-support system for activated sludge (wastewater) process control, not a task-based fMRI study with human participants. It does not involve fMRI, nor healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks.

Abstract reports behavioral factor analyses of cognitive task scores with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging methods, or task-related brain activation results. Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study.

Non-human developmental biology study in Xenopus (frog); not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Does not address the meta-analysis objective.

This is a clinical case report of a single patient; it is not an fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports clinical outcomes rather than task-related brain activation.

This is an immunology study of TSLP and macrophage polarization in allergic inflammation (mouse/human disease context). It does not involve task-based fMRI or healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks, so it is not relevant to the review.

Study involves children with ADHD (no healthy adult sample) and reports a behavioral training intervention without any fMRI data; thus it does not meet the review’s requirement for task-based fMRI in healthy adults.

This is an obstetric prediction study of neonatal metabolic acidosis using clinical/obstetric data in pregnant women; it does not involve any fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or problem-solving tasks in healthy adults. Therefore it fails the review’s imaging and population criteria.
Abstract describes a behavioral study of substitution coding in older adults with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

This is a behavioral/psychometric study of the Flynn Effect in children from NLSY data. It is not a task-based fMRI study and the sample are children (not healthy adults 18–65). Therefore it fails the review’s requirements (non-fMRI and no healthy adult sample).

This is an animal (mouse) study of pulmonary fibrosis using NK-cell depletion; not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not include healthy adult humans and is not an fMRI study, so it fails inclusion criteria.

Behavioral latent-variable study comparing working memory tasks (N=96) with no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, or task-related neural activation. The abstract reports behavioral correlations with fluid intelligence, not neuroimaging results. Fails inclusion criterion I1 (task-based fMRI) and I4 (reports brain activation), therefore excluded under E4 (non-fMRI study).

The abstract describes a behavioral study of cognitive abilities and math achievement in college students with diagnosed learning disabilities using Woodcock-Johnson tests—no task-based fMRI was used and the sample is not healthy adults. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI or healthy adult criteria.

Study tested children (clinical groups and controls), not healthy adults, and used neuropsychological behavioral testing rather than task-based fMRI. Does not meet adult healthy sample or fMRI methodology requirements.
Abstract describes molecular/targeting work for drug delivery to the choroid plexus, not a task-based fMRI study in humans performing problem-solving. No healthy adult fMRI data are reported.

This is an animal (rat) microdialysis/pharmacology study measuring serotonin release; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants performing problem-solving tasks.

The study describes a computational model and behavioral simulations with a 100-person sample; it does not report any task-based fMRI methods or task-related brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review’s fMRI inclusion criteria.

This is a qualitative case study of emergency medical dispatch and distributed cognition; it is not an fMRI study and reports no task-based brain activation data. Does not meet the requirement for task-based fMRI, so excluded.

This is an in vitro/animal lacrimal gland cell study (rabbit cells) on IL-1β and nitric oxide; it is not a task-based fMRI study, includes no human adult participants, and reports no brain activation results.

The study is biomedical cancer immunology investigating CD8+ T cells in ovarian cancer, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not include healthy adult participants nor fMRI task activation data.

This is a historical review article about membrane molecular biology, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in humans. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria for reviews/meta-analyses (E1) and non-fMRI studies (E4).

This study is a developmental text-analytic investigation of life narratives in adolescents and young adults, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving and does not report brain activation results. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI requirement for inclusion.

This is a longitudinal behavioral study of intelligence across ages 12–52 with no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, or task-related brain activation. It does not involve task-based fMRI or report activation results, so it does not meet inclusion criteria.

This is an animal (mouse) study of a drug treatment for polycystic kidney disease, not a human task-based fMRI study. It does not include healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI study reporting task-related brain activation.

This is a cross-sectional nutritional study of Indian school children aged 6–10 years, not a task-based fMRI study of adults performing problem-solving; it does not report task-related brain activation. Exclude due to no healthy adult sample and non-fMRI methodology.

Study examines structural brain morphometry, CSF biomarkers, and memory in MCI patients—not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves patient group (MCI) and no task-based fMRI activation reported.

This is a single-case neuropsychological intervention study in a former chronic drug user, not a task-based fMRI study and not a sample of healthy adults. It does not report task-related brain activation. Exclude for non-fMRI and non-healthy sample.

This is a qualitative health policy study based on interviews about NICE procedures; it is not a task-based fMRI study, reports no brain activation results, and does not involve problem-solving tasks in healthy adults. Therefore it does not meet the review’s imaging/task criteria.

Abstract describes a neuropsychological/behavioral study of children with Asperger syndrome and ADHD; no mention of fMRI or adult healthy participants. Fails inclusion I1 and I3; meets exclusions E3 and E4.
Study is about wastewater sludge monitoring using sensors and case-based reasoning; it involves no human participants and is not an fMRI study. Not relevant to task-based fMRI in healthy adults.

This is a computational/spiking neuron modeling study applied to Raven's Progressive Matrices; the abstract reports no fMRI data or human participants. Thus it is a non-fMRI study (E4) and does not include a healthy adult sample (E3).

The study involved children (clinical groups and controls) rather than healthy adults and reports neuropsychological testing, not task-based fMRI. Therefore it fails to include a healthy adult sample (E3) and is a non-fMRI study (E4).

This is an AI/methods paper on case-based reasoning for wastewater treatment; it does not involve human participants or task-based fMRI and does not report brain activation results. Therefore it does not meet the review’s requirement for fMRI studies in healthy adults.

Abstract describes behavioral experimental and psychometric modeling in 210 participants; it does not mention fMRI or any neuroimaging. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the task-based fMRI inclusion criterion.

Abstract describes psychometric structural equation modeling of WISC-IV and WIAT-II scores (achievement testing), with no mention of fMRI or neuroimaging. Participants are from a WISC-IV/WIAT-II linking sample (children), not healthy adults. Exclusion criteria E4 and E3 apply.
Participants are adolescents (mathematically gifted adolescents and control group), so the study does not include a healthy adult (18–65) sample required by the review (exclusion E3).

This study evaluates a glaucoma drainage device using in vivo (biomedical/implant) methods; it is not an fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no task-related brain activation. Exclude based on non-fMRI study and no healthy adult sample.

Abstract describes only computer-administered behavioral tasks (analytic reasoning test, dual task, Tower of Hanoi) with no mention of fMRI or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet inclusion criteria.
This is an insect-plant chemical ecology study (whiteflies and salicylic acid), not a human task-based fMRI study. It has no human participants or fMRI data, so it fails inclusion criteria.
Animal (rat) study involving molecular overexpression and alveolar fluid clearance; not a human fMRI study of problem-solving. Does not include healthy adult humans and is not an fMRI study.

This is an educational intervention study assessing problem-based writing and peer review on exam performance, with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or brain activation results. It does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

The abstract describes genetic and behavioral association studies with no mention of fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is not an fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults.

This is a clinical case report of a single patient, not an fMRI study. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing a problem-solving/reasoning task, nor does it report task-related brain activation.

Preclinical animal (rat) cancer therapy study; not a task-based fMRI investigation, contains no human participants or healthy adult sample, and reports no task-related brain activation. Exclude based on no healthy adult sample and non-fMRI study.

The study is a behavioral randomized controlled trial in older adults with no mention of fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or problem-solving tasks. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review scope.
This is an animal (murine) molecular study of hemophilia-related joint damage, not a task-based fMRI study in humans. It does not include healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI study.

Study involves children with sickle cell disease (no healthy adult sample) and reports behavioral cognitive testing of hydroxyurea effects rather than task-based fMRI or task-related brain activation. Does not meet inclusion criteria for healthy adult fMRI problem-solving studies.

This is a computational/neurofuzzy machine-learning paper applying BCM theory to online learning and S&P-500 data; it is not a task-based fMRI study, involves no human participants performing problem-solving, and reports no brain activation results.

This is a urology study of urethral sphincter responses during micturition, not an fMRI study nor a task-based problem-solving or reasoning study and does not report brain activation. Does not meet the review objective (non-fMRI).

This is an engineering/knowledge-based systems study of wastewater treatment (deflocculation); it is not a task-based fMRI study, involves no human participants or healthy adult sample, and reports no brain activation results.

This is an animal (rat) study of retinal ganglion cell injury and extracellular ATP; it is not a task-based fMRI study nor does it involve human adult participants performing problem-solving tasks. Does not meet review objectives.
Study reports neuropsychological outcomes in children/adolescents with acute lymphoblastic leukemia; no task-based fMRI is reported and there is no healthy adult (18–65) sample.

This is a psychometric evaluation of the WAIS-IV; the abstract makes no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, or task-related neural activation. It is not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults, so exclude as a non-fMRI study.
This is a behavioral time-reproduction study with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. It does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement, so exclude as a non-fMRI study.

This is an in vitro/in vivo animal neurochemistry study of taurine transport in rat hippocampal neurons, not a task-based fMRI study in human adults. It does not involve fMRI or healthy adult human participants.

This study is not an fMRI investigation of problem solving in humans: it reports materials/pharmaceutical research on chitosan films and drug release, with no human participants or task-based fMRI data.
This is a behavioral construct-validation study using factor analysis and multidimensional scaling on cognitive test scores; no task-based fMRI or brain activation results are reported.

This is a clinical case report of a postoperative complication in a single 70-year-old patient. It is not an fMRI study, does not involve a problem-solving task, does not include healthy adult participants, and reports no task-related brain activation.

This is a clinical case series of rituximab treatment in chronic fatigue syndrome patients. It is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI study) and E3 (no healthy adult sample) apply.

This is a psychometric/confirmatory factor analysis study of the WAIS-IV in adults with intellectual disabilities and matched controls. It is not a task-based fMRI study and does not report task-related brain activation, so it does not meet the fMRI inclusion criteria.

This is a proteomics study of Burkholderia mallei biomarkers, not an fMRI study of human problem-solving. It involves molecular/clinical assay development, not task-based brain imaging in healthy adults.

This is a nursing/patient-education case description of a hospitalized trauma patient, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves no fMRI, no problem-solving task, and no healthy adult sample.

Article is a pharmacology/PK-PD study in mice and humans focused on γ-secretase inhibitors for Alzheimer’s disease, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task activation or healthy adult behavioral neuroimaging data.

Participants are children with auditory brainstem implants (clinical population), not healthy adults, and the abstract does not describe any fMRI methods. Therefore it fails to meet the healthy adult (I3) and task-based fMRI (I1) requirements — exclusion criteria E3 and E4 apply.

The study used diffusion tensor imaging (structural MRI), not task-based fMRI, and reports structural integrity related to training transfer rather than task-evoked activations. Participants are described as ‘healthy elderly’ (older adults), not within the 18–65 adult range. Excluded based on E4 (non-fMRI study) and E3 (no healthy adult 18–65 sample).

This is an animal experimental study in ewes on intracerebroventricular leptin and bone loss; it is not a task-based fMRI study and includes no human adult participants. Does not meet the review’s fMRI or human-sample criteria.

This is a psychometric factor-analytic study of cognitive test batteries (KABC-II) with no mention of fMRI. The KABC-II targets children, so no healthy adult sample is indicated. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and lacks a healthy adult sample.

This is an animal (mouse) DNA vaccine immunology study, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves mice, immunological assays, and tumor models; no fMRI or human adult participants are reported.

This is a behavioral developmental study of children and adults assessing explanations/predictions about contamination; the abstract contains no mention of task-based fMRI or any neuroimaging. Therefore it fails the required fMRI criterion and should be excluded (E4).

Abstract describes behavioral study of temporal-order perception with auditory/visual tasks and cognitive predictors; no mention of task-based fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Tasks are perceptual rather than problem-solving. Excluded as a non-fMRI study (E4).
This is a historical/philosophical analysis of Boerhaave's theory of nutrition, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. No human participants or task-related brain activation data are reported.
This is a cell biology/IVF study about oocyte-like cells from follicular fluid; it does not involve task-based fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or report brain activation. Not relevant to the review.
The study describes a nanoparticle-based fluorescent glucose sensor in tear fluid — not an fMRI study, nor any task-based neuroimaging of problem solving. Exclude as a non-fMRI study.

The study reports biomarker assay results from CSF in Alzheimer's patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It lacks fMRI methods and does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks.

This is a structural MRI study of alprazolam augmentation in treatment-resistant schizophrenia patients, not a task-based fMRI study; it includes no healthy adult sample and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review objective.

This is a retrospective case series of paediatric cochlear implantation (children mean age 4.7). It is not an fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants (18–65), and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review objective.

The study is an environmental/engineering comparison of sewer systems using simulations; it is not a task-based fMRI study nor does it involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Exclusion criteria E4 and E3 apply.

This study is an engineering paper on sludge fermentation and optimization using a pilot plant; it involves no human participants and is not an fMRI study or a task-based problem-solving investigation. Therefore it does not meet the review requirements.

This is a clinical case report of a single patient with multiple cranial neuropathies; the abstract describes clinical exam and CSF findings but makes no mention of fMRI, task-based problem-solving, or healthy adult participants. Does not meet fMRI or healthy-sample criteria.
This is a theoretical physics study of colloidal suspensions (mode-coupling theory); it does not involve fMRI, human participants, or task-based brain activation. Excluded per E4 and E3.

This is an in vitro pharmacological study of indinavir nanoparticles in macrophages/HIV, not a task-based fMRI study. It has no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and does not report brain activation results.

This is a behavioural/educational study of paramedics' cannulation intentions with no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, or task-based problem-solving tasks. It therefore does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

This is a physiological study of the pressor response to water drinking in multiple-system atrophy patients; it does not describe a task-based fMRI study, nor a problem-solving/reasoning task, and does not include a healthy adult sample. Exclude based on non-fMRI modality and lack of healthy adult participants.

This paper describes a simulation/engineering extension for wastewater treatment and expert reasoning; it does not report any task-based fMRI, includes no human participants performing problem-solving tasks, nor reports brain activation results. Excluded based on non-fMRI study and no healthy adult sample.

The article is a roundtable debate/opinion piece, not an original task-based fMRI study. The abstract does not report fMRI methods or task-related brain activation, nor does it describe healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria for being a review/commentary and non-fMRI and lacking a healthy adult problem-solving sample.

This is a molecular microbiology study in Saccharomyces cerevisiae (yeast), not a human fMRI study. It has no healthy adult participants and does not involve task-based fMRI, so it fails the review criteria.

This is a clinical case report of a single patient with small bowel obstruction/gallstone ileus; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Excluded for non-fMRI methodology and no healthy adult sample.

Abstract describes behavioral assessment of depressive symptoms and cognitive functioning in middle-aged/older adults; no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Does not meet task-based fMRI requirement.

This study describes a wastewater treatment/case-based reasoning tool, not a task-based fMRI experiment. No human participants or brain imaging are reported. It therefore does not meet the fMRI or healthy adult participant criteria.

This is a clinical treatment study of diuretics in end-stage renal failure patients under palliative care, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not include healthy adult participants. Exclude based on non-fMRI methodology and lack of healthy adult sample.

This is an experimental fluid dynamics study with a flexible fibre in a soap film; it involves no human participants or fMRI methods and does not study problem solving. Exclude due to non-fMRI methods (E4) and no healthy adult sample (E3).
This is a biochemical/molecular study of a fish protein in surfperch, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. No human participants or fMRI methods are reported, so E3 and E4 apply.
This is a historical/philosophical paper on ancient medicine, not an empirical fMRI study. Abstract reports no human participants, no task-based fMRI methods, and no brain activation results.

This study describes a case-based reasoning tool for solids separation in activated sludge (engineering/computer science). It is not an fMRI study, does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review’s fMRI/problem-solving criteria.

Abstract describes longitudinal behavioral psychometric testing of cognitive abilities across cohorts; no mention of fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or brain activation results. It is not an fMRI study of problem solving, so excluded (E4).

This is an animal (rat) neurobiology study of galanin/GALP using immunohistochemistry; it is not a human task-based fMRI study and contains no healthy adult human participants. Exclusion criteria E3 (no healthy adult sample) and E4 (non-fMRI study) apply.

The study investigates eye movements and cardiopulmonary responses under simulated microgravity/antiorthostatic posture, not a task-based fMRI experiment. No problem-solving task or task-related brain activation results are reported. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and outside the review scope.

This is a molecular/clinical study of oxidative stress and NF-kappaB in bone marrow transplant patients; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and does not include healthy adult participants.

Abstract describes a biomechanical modeling study of lymph formation with no mention of fMRI, human participants, or any problem-solving/task-based paradigm. It is not an fMRI study, so it meets exclusion criterion E4.

Study is an engineering/process-control methods paper applied to a pilot-scale SBR; it does not involve human participants or fMRI-based task paradigms and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it fails inclusion criteria I1/I2/I3/I4 (no fMRI, no human problem-solving, no healthy adults).

This is an animal (mouse) study assessing P-selectin in allergic airway responses; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not include human healthy adults, and does not involve problem-solving tasks or report fMRI activations.

Participants are 8-year-old children (not adults) and the study reports a behavioral computerized spatial task with no fMRI or brain activation measures. Does not meet inclusion criteria for healthy adult sample (I3) or task-based fMRI (I1).

The study describes an environmental decision support system for wastewater treatment; it is not a task-based fMRI study, involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no brain activation results.

This is a molecular/serological study of Borrelia burgdorferi variants in Lyme disease patients; it does not involve fMRI, task-based problem-solving, or healthy adult participants. Therefore it fails key inclusion criteria.

This is a clinical case report of inhalation injury in a single patient, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It includes no healthy adult sample performing problem-solving tasks and reports no fMRI activation data.

This is a clinical/surgical case series about cavernous body reduction in patients with erectile dysfunction. It is not an fMRI study, does not involve task-based problem-solving in healthy adults, and includes patient (not healthy) participants. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria.

This is a clinical case report of acute eosinophilic pneumonia in a single patient; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving in healthy adults, and contains no healthy adult sample. Exclusion criteria E4 and E3 apply.

This is a microbiological methods study of culture techniques for hemodialysis fluids, not a task-based fMRI study. It involves water samples (no healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks) and reports no brain activation data.

Abstract is a historical/clinical discussion of kidney transplantation and serendipity, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It appears to be a review/commentary and reports no neuroimaging data.
Case report of a single patient with empyema; not an fMRI study, no task-based problem-solving paradigm, and no healthy adult sample. Does not meet the review objective.
